prompt: Use this studio worktable photo as the scene base. Create a photoreal image of a dressmaking atelier on the morning the cotton arrived: cotton fabric bolts spilling across the wooden worktable, one bolt unrolling a length of small blue-and-white floral printed cotton toward the camera, plain natural/cream cotton bolts mixed in, brown kraft paper and twine from the just-opened delivery, and a ceramic coffee mug resting on the window sill in the background. Soft warm morning window light, warm honey grade, clean and not faded. CRITICAL FOCUS REQUIREMENT: deep depth of field with the ENTIRE scene in sharp focus from the front bolts all the way back to the window — the window frame, the wall, and every object must be crisp and clearly detailed. Absolutely NO background blur, NO bokeh, NO soft focus, nothing out of focus. Emulate a large-format camera at a small aperture (about f/16) so foreground and background are equally sharp, like an interiors/editorial still-life photograph. Photorealistic, natural fabric and wood texture, fine detail throughout. No text, no logos, no watermarks. Portrait framing.
